passage_title: The Nature and Aims of Archaeology

sentences.sentence: Archaeology is partly the discovery of treasures of the past, partly the work of the scientific analyst, partly the exercise of the creative imagination.
sentences.translation: 考古学一部分是发现过去的珍宝，一部分是科学分析者的工作，也一部分是创造性想象力的运用。

structure.text: Archaeology is partly the discovery of treasures of the past, partly the work of the scientific analyst, partly the exercise of the creative imagination.

difficult_points: 先定位主语和谓语，再把介词短语或插入成分放在后面理解，句意会更清楚。
本句的并列成分需要分别对应，避免把后半句误接到错误的名词上。

sentences.sentence: It is toiling in the sun on an excavation in the Middle East, it is working with living Inuit in the snows of Alaska, and it is investigating the sewers of Roman Britain.
sentences.translation: 它可以是在中东烈日下进行发掘，也可以是在阿拉斯加的雪地里与仍在生活的因纽特人一起工作，还可以是调查罗马时期不列颠的下水道。

structure.text: It is toiling in the sun on an excavation in the Middle East, it is working with living Inuit in the snows of Alaska, and it is investigating the sewers of Roman Britain.

difficult_points: 现在分词形式在句中常作修饰或补充说明，不应一律当作新的谓语。
先定位主语和谓语，再把介词短语或插入成分放在后面理解，句意会更清楚。

sentences.sentence: But it is also the painstaking task of interpretation, so that we come to understand what these things mean for the human story.
sentences.translation: 但它同样是一项细致的阐释工作，使我们理解这些事物对于人类历史意味着什么。

structure.text: But it is also the painstaking task of interpretation, so that we come to understand what these things mean for the human story.

sentences.sentence: And it is the conservation of the world's cultural heritage against looting and careless harm.
sentences.translation: 它还是保护世界文化遗产、使其免受掠夺和粗心破坏的工作。

structure.text: And it is the conservation of the world's cultural heritage against looting and careless harm.

sentences.sentence: Archaeology, then, is both a physical activity out in the field, and an intellectual pursuit in the study or laboratory.
sentences.translation: 因此，考古学既是一种在野外开展的体力活动，也是一项在书斋或实验室中进行的智力追求。

structure.text: Archaeology, then, is both a physical activity out in the field, and an intellectual pursuit in the study or laboratory.

difficult_points: 先定位主语和谓语，再把介词短语或插入成分放在后面理解，句意会更清楚。

sentences.sentence: That is part of its great attraction.
sentences.translation: 这正是它极具吸引力的一部分原因。

structure.text: That is part of its great attraction.

difficult_points: 先定位主语和谓语，再把介词短语或插入成分放在后面理解，句意会更清楚。
本句的并列成分需要分别对应，避免把后半句误接到错误的名词上。

sentences.sentence: The rich mixture of danger and detective work has also made it the perfect vehicle for fiction writers and film-makers, from Agatha Christie with Murder in Mesopotamia to Steven Spielberg with Indiana Jones.
sentences.translation: 危险与侦探工作的丰富结合，也使它成为小说家和电影制作者的绝佳素材，从阿加莎·克里斯蒂的《美索不达米亚谋杀案》到史蒂文·斯皮尔伯格的《夺宝奇兵》皆是如此。

structure.text: The rich mixture of danger and detective work has also made it the perfect vehicle for fiction writers and film-makers, from Agatha Christie with Murder in Mesopotamia to Steven Spielberg with Indiana Jones.

sentences.sentence: However far from reality such portrayals are, they capture the essential truth that archaeology is an exciting quest - the quest for knowledge about ourselves and our past.
sentences.translation: 这些描绘无论离现实多远，都捕捉到了一个本质事实：考古学是一场令人兴奋的探索——探索有关我们自身和过去的知识。

structure.text: However far from reality such portrayals are, they capture the essential truth that archaeology is an exciting quest - the quest for knowledge about ourselves and our past.
that archaeology is an exciting quest - the quest for knowledge about ourselves and our past.

sentences.sentence: But how does archaeology relate to other disciplines such as anthropology and history that are also concerned with the human story?
sentences.translation: 不过，考古学与同样关注人类历史的人类学、历史学等学科有什么关系呢？

structure.text: But how does archaeology relate to other disciplines such as anthropology and history that are also concerned with the human story?

sentences.sentence: Is archaeology itself a science?
sentences.translation: 考古学本身是一门科学吗？

structure.text: Is archaeology itself a science?

difficult_points: 先定位主语和谓语，再把介词短语或插入成分放在后面理解，句意会更清楚。

sentences.sentence: And what are the responsibilities of the archaeologist in today's world?
sentences.translation: 在当今世界，考古学家又承担哪些责任？

structure.text: And what are the responsibilities of the archaeologist in today's world?

sentences.sentence: Anthropology, at its broadest, is the study of humanity—our physical characteristics as animals and our unique non-biological characteristics that we call culture.
sentences.translation: 从最广义看，人类学研究的是人类：既研究我们作为动物的身体特征，也研究我们称为文化的独特非生物特征。

structure.text: Anthropology, at its broadest, is the study of humanity—our physical characteristics as animals and our unique non-biological characteristics that we call culture.
that we call culture.

difficult_points: that 引导从句时通常不单独翻译，需要根据前面的动词或名词判断它是内容从句还是定语从句。
破折号后的部分多为补充说明或同位解释，阅读时先抓破折号前的主干。

sentences.sentence: Culture in this sense includes what the anthropologist, Edward Tylor, summarised in 1871 as "knowledge, beliefs, art, morals, custom and any other capabilities and habits acquired by man as a member of society".
sentences.translation: 爱德华·泰勒在 1871 年把这里的“文化”概括为“知识、信仰、艺术、道德、习俗，以及人作为社会成员所获得的其他能力和习惯”。

structure.text: Culture in this sense includes what the anthropologist, Edward Tylor, summarised in 1871 as "knowledge, beliefs, art, morals, custom and any other capabilities and habits acquired by man as a member of society".

difficult_points: 先定位主语和谓语，再把介词短语或插入成分放在后面理解，句意会更清楚。
本句的并列成分需要分别对应，避免把后半句误接到错误的名词上。

sentences.sentence: Anthropologists also use the term "culture" in a more restricted sense when they refer to the "culture" of a particular society, meaning the non-biological characteristics unique to that society, which distinguish it from other societies.
sentences.translation: 人类学家也以更狭义的方式使用“文化”一词：当他们谈论某个社会的“文化”时，指的是该社会独有、并使其有别于其他社会的非生物特征。

structure.text: Anthropologists also use the term "culture" in a more restricted sense when they refer to the "culture" of a particular society, meaning the non-biological characteristics unique to that society, which distinguish it from other societies.
which distinguish it from other societies.

difficult_points: which 引导定语从句，补充说明前面的名词或前面整个事实。

破折号后的部分多为补充说明或同位解释，阅读时先抓破折号前的主干。
不定式短语常表示目的、结果或对名词的补充，需要结合前面的谓语判断其作用。

sentences.sentence: Anthropology is thus a broad discipline—so broad that it is generally broken down into three smaller disciplines: physical anthropology, cultural anthropology and archaeology.
sentences.translation: 因此，人类学是一门范围很广的学科，通常被分为三个较小的领域：体质人类学、文化人类学和考古学。

structure.text: Anthropology is thus a broad discipline—so broad that it is generally broken down into three smaller disciplines: physical anthropology, cultural anthropology and archaeology.
that it is generally broken down into three smaller disciplines: physical anthropology

difficult_points: that 引导从句时通常不单独翻译，需要根据前面的动词或名词判断它是内容从句还是定语从句。
破折号后的部分多为补充说明或同位解释，阅读时先抓破折号前的主干。
不定式短语常表示目的、结果或对名词的补充，需要结合前面的谓语判断其作用。

sentences.sentence: Physical anthropology, or biological anthropology as it is called, concerns the study of human biological or physical characteristics and how they evolved.
sentences.translation: 体质人类学，也称生物人类学，研究人类的生物或身体特征及其演化方式。

structure.text: Physical anthropology, or biological anthropology as it is called, concerns the study of human biological or physical characteristics and how they evolved.

difficult_points: 先定位主语和谓语，再把介词短语或插入成分放在后面理解，句意会更清楚。

sentences.sentence: Cultural anthropology—or social anthropology—analyses human culture and society.
sentences.translation: 文化人类学——或称社会人类学——分析人类文化与社会。

structure.text: Cultural anthropology—or social anthropology—analyses human culture and society.

difficult_points: 破折号后的部分多为补充说明或同位解释，阅读时先抓破折号前的主干。
先定位主语和谓语，再把介词短语或插入成分放在后面理解，句意会更清楚。

sentences.sentence: Two of its branches are ethnography (the study at first hand of individual living cultures) and ethnology (which sets out to compare cultures using ethnographic evidence to derive general principles about human society).
sentences.translation: 它的两个分支是民族志学（亲身研究个别仍在存续的文化）和民族学（利用民族志证据比较各种文化，以归纳人类社会的一般原则）。

structure.text: Two of its branches are ethnography (the study at first hand of individual living cultures) and ethnology (which sets out to compare cultures using ethnographic evidence to derive general principles about human society).
which sets out to compare cultures using ethnographic evidence to derive general principles about human society).

difficult_points: which 引导定语从句，补充说明前面的名词或前面整个事实。

现在分词形式在句中常作修饰或补充说明，不应一律当作新的谓语。

sentences.sentence: Archaeology is the "past tense of cultural anthropology".
sentences.translation: 考古学是“文化人类学的过去时”。

structure.text: Archaeology is the "past tense of cultural anthropology".

difficult_points: 先定位主语和谓语，再把介词短语或插入成分放在后面理解，句意会更清楚。
本句的并列成分需要分别对应，避免把后半句误接到错误的名词上。

sentences.sentence: Whereas cultural anthropologists will often base their conclusions on the experience of living within contemporary communities, archaeologists study past societies primarily through their material remains—the buildings, tools, and other artefacts that constitute what is known as the material culture left over from former societies.
sentences.translation: 文化人类学家往往依据自己在当代社群中生活的经历得出结论，而考古学家主要通过过去社会留下的物质遗存——建筑、工具和其他构成所谓物质文化的人工制品——来研究它们。

structure.text: Whereas cultural anthropologists will often base their conclusions on the experience of living within contemporary communities, archaeologists study past societies primarily through their material remains—the buildings, tools, and other artefacts that constitute what is known as the material culture left over from former societies.
that constitute what is known as the material culture left over from former societies.

difficult_points: that 引导从句时通常不单独翻译，需要根据前面的动词或名词判断它是内容从句还是定语从句。
破折号后的部分多为补充说明或同位解释，阅读时先抓破折号前的主干。

sentences.sentence: Nevertheless, one of the most important tasks for the archaeologist today is to know how to interpret material culture in human terms.
sentences.translation: 然而，当今考古学家最重要的任务之一，是懂得怎样从人的角度诠释物质文化。

structure.text: Nevertheless, one of the most important tasks for the archaeologist today is to know how to interpret material culture in human terms.

difficult_points: 不定式短语常表示目的、结果或对名词的补充，需要结合前面的谓语判断其作用。

sentences.sentence: How were those pots used?
sentences.translation: 那些陶罐是怎样使用的？

structure.text: How were those pots used?

difficult_points: 先定位主语和谓语，再把介词短语或插入成分放在后面理解，句意会更清楚。
本句的并列成分需要分别对应，避免把后半句误接到错误的名词上。

sentences.sentence: Why are some dwellings round and others square?
sentences.translation: 为什么有些居所是圆形的，另一些却是方形的？

structure.text: Why are some dwellings round and others square?

difficult_points: 现在分词形式在句中常作修饰或补充说明，不应一律当作新的谓语。

sentences.sentence: Here the methods of archaeology and ethnography overlap.
sentences.translation: 在这里，考古学与民族志学的方法相互重叠。

structure.text: Here the methods of archaeology and ethnography overlap.

difficult_points: 先定位主语和谓语，再把介词短语或插入成分放在后面理解，句意会更清楚。
本句的并列成分需要分别对应，避免把后半句误接到错误的名词上。

sentences.sentence: Archaeologists in recent decades have developed "ethnoarchaeology", where, like ethnographers, they live among contemporary communities, but with the specific purpose of learning how such societies use material culture—how they make their tools and weapons, why they build their settlements where they do, and so on.
sentences.translation: 近几十年来，考古学家发展出“民族考古学”：他们像民族志学家一样生活在当代社群中，但具体目的是了解这类社会怎样使用物质文化——怎样制作工具和武器、为什么在特定地点建聚落，等等。

structure.text: Archaeologists in recent decades have developed "ethnoarchaeology", where, like ethnographers, they live among contemporary communities, but with the specific purpose of learning how such societies use material culture—how they make their tools and weapons, why they build their settlements where they do, and so on.
where they do

现在分词形式在句中常作修饰或补充说明，不应一律当作新的谓语。

sentences.sentence: Moreover, archaeology has a role to play in the field of conservation.
sentences.translation: 此外，考古学在保护领域也能发挥作用。

structure.text: Moreover, archaeology has a role to play in the field of conservation.

difficult_points: 不定式短语常表示目的、结果或对名词的补充，需要结合前面的谓语判断其作用。
先定位主语和谓语，再把介词短语或插入成分放在后面理解，句意会更清楚。

sentences.sentence: Heritage studies constitute a developing field, where it is realised that the world's cultural heritage is a diminishing resource which holds different meanings for different people.
sentences.translation: 遗产研究正在成为一个发展中的领域，人们已认识到世界文化遗产是一种不断减少的资源，对不同的人有不同的意义。

structure.text: Heritage studies constitute a developing field, where it is realised that the world's cultural heritage is a diminishing resource which holds different meanings for different people.
which holds different meanings for different people.

difficult_points: which 引导定语从句，补充说明前面的名词或前面整个事实。
that 引导从句时通常不单独翻译，需要根据前面的动词或名词判断它是内容从句还是定语从句。
现在分词形式在句中常作修饰或补充说明，不应一律当作新的谓语。

sentences.sentence: If, then, archaeology deals with the past, in what way does it differ from history?
sentences.translation: 那么，如果考古学处理的是过去，它与历史又在哪方面不同？

structure.text: If, then, archaeology deals with the past, in what way does it differ from history?

difficult_points: 先定位主语和谓语，再把介词短语或插入成分放在后面理解，句意会更清楚。
本句的并列成分需要分别对应，避免把后半句误接到错误的名词上。

sentences.sentence: In the broadest sense, just as archaeology is an aspect of anthropology, so too is it a part of history—where we mean the whole history of humankind from its beginnings over three million years ago.
sentences.translation: 从最广的意义上说，正如考古学是人类学的一个方面，它也是历史的一部分——这里的历史指从三百多万年前起整个人类的历史。

structure.text: In the broadest sense, just as archaeology is an aspect of anthropology, so too is it a part of history—where we mean the whole history of humankind from its beginnings over three million years ago.
where we mean the whole history of humankind from its beginnings over three million years ago.

difficult_points: 破折号后的部分多为补充说明或同位解释，阅读时先抓破折号前的主干。
现在分词形式在句中常作修饰或补充说明，不应一律当作新的谓语。

sentences.sentence: Indeed, for more than ninety-nine percent of that huge span of time, archaeology—the study of past material culture—is the only significant source of information.
sentences.translation: 实际上，在那段极其漫长的时期中，超过百分之九十九的时间里，考古学——对过去物质文化的研究——是唯一重要的信息来源。

structure.text: Indeed, for more than ninety-nine percent of that huge span of time, archaeology—the study of past material culture—is the only significant source of information.
that huge span of time

difficult_points: that 引导从句时通常不单独翻译，需要根据前面的动词或名词判断它是内容从句还是定语从句。
破折号后的部分多为补充说明或同位解释，阅读时先抓破折号前的主干。

sentences.sentence: Conventional historical sources begin only with the introduction of written records around 3,000 BC in western Asia, and much later in most other parts of the world.
sentences.translation: 传统的历史资料只从西亚约公元前 3000 年书面记录出现时才开始，而世界大多数其他地区要晚得多。

structure.text: Conventional historical sources begin only with the introduction of written records around 3,000 BC in western Asia, and much later in most other parts of the world.

difficult_points: 先定位主语和谓语，再把介词短语或插入成分放在后面理解，句意会更清楚。
本句的并列成分需要分别对应，避免把后半句误接到错误的名词上。

sentences.sentence: A commonly drawn distinction is between prehistory, i.e. the period before written records, and history in the narrow sense, meaning the study of the past using written evidence.
sentences.translation: 常见的区分是：史前时代，即有文字记录之前的时期；以及狭义的历史，即利用书面证据研究过去。

structure.text: A commonly drawn distinction is between prehistory, i.e. the period before written records, and history in the narrow sense, meaning the study of the past using written evidence.

difficult_points: 现在分词形式在句中常作修饰或补充说明，不应一律当作新的谓语。
先定位主语和谓语，再把介词短语或插入成分放在后面理解，句意会更清楚。

sentences.sentence: To archaeology, which studies all cultures and periods, whether with or without writing, the distinction between history and prehistory is a convenient dividing line that recognises the importance of the written word, but in no way lessens the importance of the useful information contained in oral histories.
sentences.translation: 对于研究所有文化和时期、无论有无文字的考古学而言，历史与史前史的划分只是一个便利的分界线：它承认文字的重要性，却丝毫没有降低口述历史中有用信息的重要性。

structure.text: To archaeology, which studies all cultures and periods, whether with or without writing, the distinction between history and prehistory is a convenient dividing line that recognises the importance of the written word, but in no way lessens the importance of the useful information contained in oral histories.
which studies all cultures and periods

difficult_points: which 引导定语从句，补充说明前面的名词或前面整个事实。
that 引导从句时通常不单独翻译，需要根据前面的动词或名词判断它是内容从句还是定语从句。
现在分词形式在句中常作修饰或补充说明，不应一律当作新的谓语。

sentences.sentence: Since the aim of archaeology is the understanding of humankind, it is a humanistic study, and since it deals with the human past, it is a historical discipline.
sentences.translation: 由于考古学的目标是理解人类，它是一门人文学科；又由于它处理人类过去，它也是一门历史学科。

structure.text: Since the aim of archaeology is the understanding of humankind, it is a humanistic study, and since it deals with the human past, it is a historical discipline.
Since the aim of archaeology is the understanding of humankind

difficult_points: 现在分词形式在句中常作修饰或补充说明，不应一律当作新的谓语。
先定位主语和谓语，再把介词短语或插入成分放在后面理解，句意会更清楚。

sentences.sentence: But it differs from the study of written history in a fundamental way.
sentences.translation: 但它与书面历史研究在根本上有所不同。

structure.text: But it differs from the study of written history in a fundamental way.

difficult_points: 先定位主语和谓语，再把介词短语或插入成分放在后面理解，句意会更清楚。
本句的并列成分需要分别对应，避免把后半句误接到错误的名词上。

sentences.sentence: The material the archaeologist finds does not tell us directly what to think.
sentences.translation: 考古学家发现的材料并不直接告诉我们应该如何思考。

structure.text: The material the archaeologist finds does not tell us directly what to think.

difficult_points: 不定式短语常表示目的、结果或对名词的补充，需要结合前面的谓语判断其作用。
先定位主语和谓语，再把介词短语或插入成分放在后面理解，句意会更清楚。

sentences.sentence: Historical records make statements, offer opinions and pass judgements.
sentences.translation: 历史记录会作出陈述、提出观点并下判断。

structure.text: Historical records make statements, offer opinions and pass judgements.

difficult_points: 先定位主语和谓语，再把介词短语或插入成分放在后面理解，句意会更清楚。
本句的并列成分需要分别对应，避免把后半句误接到错误的名词上。

sentences.sentence: The objects the archaeologists discover, on the other hand, tell us nothing directly in themselves.
sentences.translation: 相反，考古学家发现的物件本身并不会直接告诉我们任何事情。

structure.text: The objects the archaeologists discover, on the other hand, tell us nothing directly in themselves.

difficult_points: 现在分词形式在句中常作修饰或补充说明，不应一律当作新的谓语。
先定位主语和谓语，再把介词短语或插入成分放在后面理解，句意会更清楚。

sentences.sentence: In this respect, the practice of the archaeologist is rather like that of the scientist, who collects data, conducts experiments, formulates a hypothesis, tests the hypothesis against more data, and then, in conclusion, devises a model that seems best to summarise the pattern observed in the data.
sentences.translation: 在这一点上，考古学家的实践很像科学家：科学家收集数据、做实验、提出假设、用更多数据检验假设，最后建立一个似乎最能概括所观察数据模式的模型。

structure.text: In this respect, the practice of the archaeologist is rather like that of the scientist, who collects data, conducts experiments, formulates a hypothesis, tests the hypothesis against more data, and then, in conclusion, devises a model that seems best to summarise the pattern observed in the data.
that of the scientist

difficult_points: that 引导从句时通常不单独翻译，需要根据前面的动词或名词判断它是内容从句还是定语从句。
不定式短语常表示目的、结果或对名词的补充，需要结合前面的谓语判断其作用。

sentences.sentence: The archaeologist has to develop a picture of the past, just as the scientist has to develop a coherent view of the natural world.
sentences.translation: 考古学家必须构建一幅过去的图景，正如科学家必须形成对自然世界连贯的看法。

structure.text: The archaeologist has to develop a picture of the past, just as the scientist has to develop a coherent view of the natural world.

difficult_points: 不定式短语常表示目的、结果或对名词的补充，需要结合前面的谓语判断其作用。
先定位主语和谓语，再把介词短语或插入成分放在后面理解，句意会更清楚。
